title: High Crude Prices To Bring Down GDP Growth To 5.8 pc, Monsoon To Pull Down Further
description: Historically, India has experienced El Niño in 8 of the last 24 years—roughly once every three years. In those years, monsoon rainfall ranged from 1 to 22 per cent below normal. Kharif output also declined in all those instances, with contractions ranging between 1 and 23 per cent depending on the intensity of El Niño
